title: Thousands of Hartenbos River fish dead
description: MOSSEL BAY NEWS - Thousands of dead fish...
bodyText: [{"type":"paragraph","text":"MOSSEL BAY NEWS - Thousands of dead fish from the Hartenbos River washed up along the Hartenbos Lagoon yesterday  morning, 2 January.","html":"MOSSEL BAY NEWS - Thousands of dead fish from the Hartenbos River washed up along the Hartenbos Lagoon yesterday morning, 2 January."},{"type":"blockquote","text":"Drone Fanatics SA captured video footage and photographs of the masses of dead fish along the shoreline earlier today."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Phillip Vermaak, the chairperson of the River Forum, told Mossel Bay Advertiser that he had already been out to the scene today, 3 January and spoken with authorities who arranged with a contractor for a clean-up of the fish.","html":"Phillip Vermaak, the chairperson of the River Forum, told Mossel Bay Advertiser that he had already been out to the scene today, 3 January and spoken with authorities who arranged with a contractor for a clean-up of the fish."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Vermaak said the death of thousands of fish from the Hartenbos River is expected to repeat every few years, whenever there is a heatwave due to how shallow the river currently is.","html":"Vermaak said the death of thousands of fish from the Hartenbos River is expected to repeat every few years, whenever there is a heatwave due to how shallow the river currently is."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"He said there were a few factors that would have contributed to the deaths but the main one is that the river has gone from 1.9 mean level to 0.7.","html":"He said there were a few factors that would have contributed to the deaths but the main one is that the river has gone from 1.9 mean level to 0.7."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Vermaak said he took a reading near the surface of the river yesterday afternoon, 2 January and in some of the shallower parts it was 34 degrees Celsius.","html":"Vermaak said he took a reading near the surface of the river yesterday afternoon, 2 January and in some of the shallower parts it was 34 degrees Celsius."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Drone Fanatics SA captured this footage of the dead fish along the shoreline","html":"Drone Fanatics SA captured this footage of the dead fish along the shoreline"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Your browser does not support HTML5 video.","html":""},{"type":"paragraph","text":"He said the water in deeper parts was 24 degrees Celsius yesterday evening. Vermaak said these temperatures are for tropical fish, not the fish in the Hartenbos River.","html":"He said the water in deeper parts was 24 degrees Celsius yesterday evening. Vermaak said these temperatures are for tropical fish, not the fish in the Hartenbos River."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Vermaak added that whenever there is a heatwave, the high temperatures cause the sludge and algae in the water to bloom, a process called eutrophication. This rids the water of oxygen and causes the fish to suffocate and ultimately die.","html":"Vermaak added that whenever there is a heatwave, the high temperatures cause the sludge and algae in the water to bloom, a process called eutrophication. This rids the water of oxygen and causes the fish to suffocate and ultimately die."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"He said the shallowness of the water adds to the rapid heating of the river.","html":"He said the shallowness of the water adds to the rapid heating of the river."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"The dead fish washed ashore this morning, 3 January. Photo: Drone Fanatics SA","html":"The dead fish washed ashore this morning, 3 January. Photo: Drone Fanatics SA"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"\"The only solution I see is to dredge the river and make it deeper,\" he said.","html":"\"The only solution I see is to dredge the river and make it deeper,\" he said."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"The Mossel Bay Municipality said on a post on its Facebook page that it is aware of dead fish that washed up at the mouth of the Hartenbos River.","html":"The Mossel Bay Municipality said on a post on its Facebook page that it is aware of dead fish that washed up at the mouth of the Hartenbos River."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"It said in a post today that scientific observations have confirmed that the fish kill resulted from severe oxygen depletion within the estuary.","html":"It said in a post today that scientific observations have confirmed that the fish kill resulted from severe oxygen depletion within the estuary."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"\"The oxygen deficit was caused by an algal bloom, exacerbated by a combination of low water levels, elevated temperatures, and nutrient-rich conditions - all factors known to accelerate oxygen consumption and create hypoxic conditions,\" it said.","html":"\"The oxygen deficit was caused by an algal bloom, exacerbated by a combination of low water levels, elevated temperatures, and nutrient-rich conditions - all factors known to accelerate oxygen consumption and create hypoxic conditions,\" it said."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Questions have been sent to the Western Cape Department of Forestry, Fisheries and the Environment. It's response will be added as soon as it is received.","html":"Questions have been sent to the Western Cape Department of Forestry, Fisheries and the Environment. It's response will be added as soon as it is received."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’","html":"‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’"}]
